reasoning: The user requested a 'fresh mobile-optimized look', migrating away from a dark brutalist web aesthetic. For an 8-month intensive engineering grind, a soft pastel palette combined with high-contrast sharp boundaries (Neo-Brutalism) prevents visual fatigue, while chunky tactile components make checking off daily tasks highly satisfying.

typography.anti_default_rule: Avoid generic sans-serifs for headings. Used highly legible, modern geometric sans.

spacing_and_layout.philosophy: Generous Spacing & Tactile Cards. Do not cram elements; use the full vertical real estate of the mobile screen.

task_card.style: bg-white rounded-3xl p-6 border-2 border-slate-100 shadow-sm
task_card.description: The main interaction point. Needs to feel chunky and 'pressable'. Must contain the stream pill, task title, and a large 'Upload Proof' button or image thumbnail.

master_progress_bar.description: A thick, highly visible progress bar fixed near the top of the Home or Analytics view.

streak_counter.style: flex flex-row items-center gap-2 bg-amber-100 px-4 py-2 rounded-full border border-amber-200
streak_counter.description: Should use a flame icon. Display prominently on the top header.

navigation_bottom_sheet.description: Instead of a web sidebar, use a bottom sheet or standard bottom tab navigator to access Weeks/Months.

filter_pills.style: px-5 py-2 rounded-full border border-slate-200 bg-white font-semibold text-sm

motion_and_interactions.press_state: All buttons and cards must have a scale-down effect (e.g., scale: 0.98) on press to mimic physical buttons.
motion_and_interactions.completion_animation: When a photo is uploaded and a task is marked done, the card background should transition smoothly to emerald-50 and trigger a brief haptic feedback (if possible in RN) and a checkmark pop-in.
motion_and_interactions.list_entrance: Stagger the entrance of the 4 daily task cards when the day loads.

icons_and_assets.library: Phosphor Icons (Duotone) or Lucide (Stroke width 2.5 for thicker, friendlier appearance)
icons_and_assets.usage_rules: Use large, bold icons. Avoid thin, clinical iconography.

image_urls.description: Minimalist geometric shapes against a clear pastel sky.
Creative arrangement of colorful geometric shapes against a white background.

media.rules: Use these images for generic motivational banners at the top of the Analytics or Weekly Summary pages. Do not use placeholder URLs anywhere.

instructions_to_main_agent: You are building a React Native Expo app. Translate the Tailwind utility concepts provided in these guidelines into your React Native styling solution (e.g., NativeWind or StyleSheet).
Replace the Web-based 'Sidebar' with a Mobile-Native Navigation pattern: use a Bottom Tab Navigator for main views (Today, Analytics, Backlog) and a Bottom Sheet (or modal) for Month/Week selection.
For the 'Photo Proof' requirement, implement an image picker/camera modal that attaches to the Task Card.
The 4 Task Cards (DSA, Backend, Data, Cloud) are the core of the app. Render them as large 'Bento' style blocks (refer to components.task_card in this JSON).
Ensure the layout strictly follows the generous padding rules (px-6 py-4 for screens, rounded-3xl for cards). Avoid standard 'Bootstrap/Vercel' look; lean into the Neo-brutalist tactile aesthetic.
Every interactive element must include 'testID' attributes (the RN equivalent of data-testid) formatted in kebab-case (e.g., testID='upload-proof-button').
Implement the 'Day Locking Mechanism' by graying out future days and disabling interactions, visually distinct from pending or completed tasks.
